title: Facilitator Guide — Day 1: Stories, Identity & Squad Formation
prepChecklist: Load Program Coach Deck 01 and Deck 02 — confirm speaker notes visible in program coach view
Confirm squad assignments and mentor pairings in formation admin
Test Venture Blueprint Day 1 Builders access for all participants
Print or share evaluation templates: participation rubric, venture identity rubric, program coach observation form
Brief mentors on Day 1 mentor guide and observation areas
Prepare dream board materials (digital studio + optional physical supplies)

sessionFlow.activity: Program Coach Deck 01 — AIA, Agency, SPIKE Stories
sessionFlow.notes: Deliver all six Deck 01 slides with stories, not bullet reads. Use discussion questions on slides 2–4.

sessionFlow.activity: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future (overview + Ambition + Impact)
sessionFlow.notes: Slides 1–3. Transition directly into ambition and impact builders.
Individual work with mentor circulation. Debrief each activity using activity guide questions.
Mentors check squad roster and preview afternoon flow.

sessionFlow.activity: Deck 02 — Future Self + Career Direction slides + builders
sessionFlow.notes: Slides 5–6 then activities. Dyad discussion for career direction.
Squads form visually then confirm membership in app. Gallery walk at 2:55.
Digital signatures required. Program Coach and mentor sign-off.
Program Coach uses observation form and venture identity rubric. Participation assessment in app.
Participant reflection survey. Mentors complete debrief reflection form with program coach.

debriefQuestions: Which squad dream boards showed strongest alignment between personal ambition and venture thesis?
Where did participants struggle to distinguish entrepreneurship from traditional employment?
Which identity builder produced the most authentic voice — and which produced the most generic answers?
What coaching will mentors provide before Day 2 industry immersion?
Are all Day 1 deliverables visible in Venture Portfolio for every participant?
commonPitfalls: Rushing Deck 01 stories — participants need emotional frame before builders
Reading slides instead of telling AIA and agency stories
Skipping debrief questions after each builder activity
Treating identity builders as forms to rush through
Allowing squad charter without assigned roles or digital signatures
coachingTips: Program Coaches and mentors use the same language: ambition, impact, values, future self, career direction
Link every activity output to Portfolio Identity and Business Plan Chapter 1
Use Week 1 Journey panel in Venture Portfolio as live progress check
Flag participants with observation form 'needs follow-up' for mentor priority on Day 2
